vulnerability.title: Add nosuid Option to /boot/efi
vulnerability.description: The nosuid mount option can be used to prevent execution of setuid programs in /boot/efi. The SUID and SGID permissions should not be required on the boot partition. Add the nosuid option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /boot/efi.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ) && { findmnt --kernel "/boot/efi" > /dev/null || findmnt --fstab "/boot/efi" > /dev/null; }; then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /boot/efi has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/boot/efi")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo

triage_decision.reason: Adding the nosuid option to the /boot/efi mount is a straightforward configuration change that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services. It follows standard STIG hardening practices and is unlikely to lock out users or break the system.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if /boot/efi is misconfigured; Requires reboot to take effect

Implementation: Use `awk` to process each line:

```
awk '
{
    if ($2 == "/boot/efi") {
        # uncomment line removing leading # if present
        sub(/^#\s*/,"");
        # parse comment part
        n = split($0, a, "#");
        if (n>1) comment = "#" a[2]; else comment = "";
        # process mount options column ($4)
        split($4, opts, ",");
        hasnosuid=0;
        for (i in opts) if (opts[i]=="nosuid") hasnosuid=1;
        if (!hasnosuid) $4=$4",nosuid";
        # print line with fields 1-5 and comment
        print $1, $2, $3, $4, $5 comment;
        next;
    }
}
{print}
' /etc/fstab > /etc/fstab.tmp && mv /etc/fstab.tmp /etc/fstab
```

Let's craft the awk script:

```
awk '
{
    # If line starts with optional #, we want to consider un-commenting if contains /boot/efi
    line=$0;
    # if leading comment char, remove it for checking mountpoint
    temp=line;
    sub(/^#\s*/,"",temp);
    if (temp ~ /\/boot\/efi/) {
        # remove leading # if present
        sub(/^#\s*/,"",line);
        # split into fields
        n=split(line, fields, "[ \t]+");
        # fields: 1 filesystem, 2 mountpoint, 3 fstype, 4 options, 5 dump, 6 pass
        # handle comment part
        sub(/^([^#]*)#.*/, "\\1", line);  # remove comment to get fields properly? easier: use previous split
        # Actually we can split by [ \t]+ but comment might be attached to last field; need to separate.
        # We split line by # to remove comment
        split(line, parts, "#");
        line=parts[1];
        comment="";
        if (length(parts)>1) comment="#" parts[2];
        split(line, fields, "[ \t]+");
        if (length(fields)>=4) {
            hasnosuid=0;
            split(fields[4], opts, ",");
            for (i in opts) if (opts[i]=="nosuid") hasnosuid=1;
            if (!hasnosuid) fields[4]=fields[4]",nosuid";
            # rebuild line
            out=fields[1]" "fields[2]" "fields[3]" "fields[4]"";
            # add remaining fields if any
            for (i=5; i<=length(fields); i++) out=out" "fields[i];
            if (comment!="") out=out" "comment;
            print out;
        } else{print line}
        next;
    }
}
{print}
' /etc/fstab > /tmp/fstab.tmp
```

In awk, for lines where $2 == "/boot/efi", we do:

```
if ($2 == "/boot/efi") {
   sub(/^#\s*/,"");
   # capture comment part separately:
   comment = "";
   if (match($0, /#.*$/)) { match($0, /#.*$/); comment = substr($0, RSTART); $0 = substr($0, 1, RSTART-1); }
   # remove leading/trailing spaces
   sub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/,"");
   split($0, fields, "[ \t]+");
   # fields[4] is options
   split(fields[4], opts, ",");
   cnt=0;
   for (i in opts) if (opts[i]=="nosuid") cnt++; 
   if (cnt==0) fields[4] = fields[4]","nosuid;
   printf "%s %s %s %s %s %s%s\n", fields[1],fields[2],fields[3],fields[4],fields[5],fields[6],comment;
   next;
}
```

review_feedback: The proposed sed expression captures only the part of the options field before the first comma, potentially discarding existing options (e.g., ro, defaults, noatime). This can inadvertently change the mount behavior of /boot/efi (e.g., making it writable) and introduces additional security risk. Additionally, the plan does not remount the filesystem, so the change will only take effect after a reboot, and the script does not check for the presence of the mount entry before editing. The approach is therefore not safe or fully idempotent.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed sed expression captures only the part of the options field before the first comma, potentially discarding existing options (e.g., ro, defaults, noatime). This can inadvertently change the mount behavior of /boot/efi (e.g., making it writable) and introduces additional security risk. Additionally, the plan does not remount the filesystem, so the change will only take effect after a reboot, and the script does not check for the presence of the mount entry before editing. The approach is therefore not safe or fully idempotent.
concerns: May truncate existing option list, altering mount characteristics.
Does not remount /boot/efi after editing, relying only on reboot.
Potential for syntax errors on non-standard /etc/fstab entries.
suggested_improvements: Use a regex that preserves the entire options field: e.g., sed -i -E '/\/boot\/efi/ s#(^[^ ]+\s+[^ ]+\s+[^ ]+\s+)([^,]*)(.*$)#\1\2,nosuid\3#' which appends nosuid without removing previous options.
Add a check to ensure the /boot/efi line exists before editing – abort if not present.
After editing fstab, run 'mount -o remount /boot/efi' to apply change immediately, or document the need to reboot.
Test the resulting fstab with 'mount -l /boot/efi' to confirm all options apply correctly.
Provide a backup of /etc/fstab before modifying (e.g., cp /etc/fstab /etc/fstab.bak).
